ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the country belongs */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency used in the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language(s) of the country */,
  "geography" TEXT /* Geographical information (e.g., area, climate) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "happiness_report" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the report */,
  "happiness_rank" INTEGER /* Happiness ranking of the country */,
  "happiness_score" REAL /* Happiness score of the country */,
  "standard_error" REAL /* Standard error associated with the happiness score */,
  "economy_gdp" REAL /* GDP per capita as an economic indicator */,
  "family_support" REAL /* Social support or family score */,
  "health_life_expectancy" REAL /* Life expectancy as a health indicator */,
  "freedom" REAL /* Freedom to make life choices */,
  "trust_government" REAL /* Perceptions of corruption in government */,
  "generosity" REAL /* Generosity of the population */,
  "dystopia_residual" REAL /* Dystopia residual score */,
  "total_population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "urban_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of population living in urban areas */,
  "rural_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of population living in rural areas */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate */,
  "inflation_rate" REAL /* Inflation rate */,
  "poverty_rate" REAL /* Poverty rate */,
  "education_index" REAL /* Education index */,
  "healthcare_quality_index" REAL /* Healthcare quality index */,
  "environmental_quality_index" REAL /* Environmental quality index */,
  "social_inequality_index" REAL /* Social inequality index */,
  "migration_rate" REAL /* Migration rate (in and out) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_trust_government FOREIGN KEY ("trust_government") REFERENCES socioeconomic_factors ("factor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_economy_gdp FOREIGN KEY ("economy_gdp") REFERENCES socioeconomic_factors ("factor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_freedom FOREIGN KEY ("freedom") REFERENCES socioeconomic_factors ("factor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_family_support FOREIGN KEY ("family_support") REFERENCES socioeconomic_factors ("factor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_generosity FOREIGN KEY ("generosity") REFERENCES socioeconomic_factors ("factor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_happiness_report_health_life_expectancy FOREIGN KEY ("health_life_expectancy") REFERENCES socioeconomic_factors ("factor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "socioeconomic_factors" (
  "factor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each socioeconomic factor */,
  "factor_name" TEXT /* Name of the factor */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the factor */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the factor */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "relevance_to_happiness" TEXT /* Brief description of how the factor relates to happiness */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last date the factor data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("factor_id")
)
column_names: Region to which the country belongs
Unique identifier for each report entry
Standard error associated with the happiness score
GDP per capita as an economic indicator
Life expectancy as a health indicator
Percentage of population living in urban areas
Percentage of population living in rural areas
Unique identifier for each socioeconomic factor
Unit of measurement for the factor
Brief description of how the factor relates to happiness
Last date the factor data was updated
